basic_details.job_title: NIRDPR Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online for 14 Junior Fellow, Research Associates and More Posts
basic_details.organization_name: National Institute of Rural Development and Panchayati Raj (NIRDPR)
basic_details.short_information: National Institute of Rural Development and Panchayati Raj (NIRDPR) has released an official notification for the recruitment of 14 Junior Fellow Posts. Interested and eligible candidates can apply Online through the official NIRDPR website. The last date to submit the application form is 11-05-2026.

age_limit.details: The maximum age limit for all posts is 57 years.

qualification.education_qualification: Post-Graduate in relevant fields or Graduate/Post-Graduate in Civil Engineering, Construction Management, or allied disciplines. For Accountant roles, Post Graduate in Commerce or Finance / Masters in Business Administration (Specialization in Finance) or CA inter.
qualification.details: Junior Fellows (Capacity Building): Post-Graduate in Rural Development, Social Sciences, Social Work, Agriculture, or allied fields, Environment Science. Minimum 5 years of work experience in training, capacity building, or rural development projects. Junior Fellows Works (NRM Convergence): Post-Graduate in Agriculture, Social Sciences, Animal Husbandry, Horticulture and Forestry, Rural Development, Social Work or Economics. Minimum 5 years of work experience in programme convergence, rural planning, or inter-scheme coordination. Experience in Mahatma Gandhi NREGA or rural livelihood programs with multi-scheme integration. Junior Fellows (Infrastructure): Graduate / Post-Graduate in Civil Engineering, Construction Management, or allied disciplines. Minimum 5 years of work experience in rural infrastructure planning, supervision, or implementation. Junior Fellows (Social Audit): Post-Graduate in Social Work, Sociology, Public Administration, Political Science, Rural Development or allied fields. Minimum 5 years of work experience in social accountability, community mobilization, or governance programs. Research Associates: Post Graduate degree in Statistics, Social Sciences, Economics, Social Work and Rural Development. Minimum 3 years of work experience in Govt. projects. 2 years of research experience under Mahatma Gandhi NREGS projects. Academic Associates (Capacity Building): Post Graduate in Agriculture Economics, Animal Husbandry, Horticulture and Forestry, Social Sciences/Rural Development & Social Work. Minimum 3 years of work experience in Govt. sector. At least 2 years of experience in coordinating Training programmes under Mahatma Gandhi NREGS. Academic Associates (Infrastructure): Graduate / Post-Graduate in Civil Engineering, Construction Management, or allied disciplines. Minimum 3 years of work experience in rural infrastructure planning, supervision, or implementation. Academic Associates (Social Audit): Post-Graduate in Social Work, Sociology, Public Administration, Political Science, Rural Development or allied fields. Minimum 3 years of work experience in social accountability, community mobilization, or governance programs. Junior Accountant (Finance): Post Graduate in Commerce or finance / Masters in Business Administration (Specialization in Finance) or CA inter. Minimum 3 years of work experience in office administration and accounting. At least 2 years in Government office. Senior Accountant cum Administrator (Finance): Post Graduate degree in Finance / Business Administration with specialization in Accounts / Finance / CA inter. Minimum 10 years of work experience in the Government Sector in Office Administration and Accounting.

how_to_apply: Applications must be submitted online on or before 11-05-2026.
To apply directly, use the Apply Online link provided in the Important Links section.
Alternatively, candidates may visit the official website of NIRDPR to complete the application.
Fill out the application form carefully with correct personal, educational, and contact details.
Upload required documents such as photograph, signature, and certificates in the prescribed format.
Pay the application fee online, if applicable.
Review all details carefully before submitting the form.
Take a printout or save a copy of the submitted application for future reference.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for the NIRDPR recruitment?
faq.answer: The last date to submit the online application for the NIRDPR recruitment is 11 May 2026. Ensure your application is submitted well before this deadline.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are available in NIRDPR recruitment?
faq.answer: There are a total of 14 vacancies announced for various posts, including Junior Fellows, Research Associates, Academic Associates, and Accountants, in the NIRDPR recruitment drive.

faq.question: Q3. What is the minimum qualification required for the Junior Fellow posts?
faq.answer: The minimum qualification for Junior Fellow posts is a Post-Graduate degree in relevant fields such as Rural Development, Social Sciences, Social Work, Agriculture, or allied disciplines. Specific requirements vary by specialization.

faq.question: Q4. What is the age limit for applying to NIRDPR posts?
faq.answer: The maximum age limit for all the posts announced in this NIRDPR recruitment is 57 years. There is no mention of minimum age limit in the provided data.

faq.question: Q5. What is the salary structure for the recruited candidates?
faq.answer: The salary for the recruited candidates ranges from Rs. 60,000/- to Rs. 1,20,000/- per month, depending on the post. Junior Fellows and Senior Accountants receive higher remuneration.

faq.question: Q6. What is the application fee for NIRDPR recruitment?
faq.answer: The application fee for General/OBC/EWS candidates is Rs. 300/- plus applicable taxes. SC/ST/PWD candidates are exempted from paying any application fee.

faq.question: Q7. What is the mode of application for NIRDPR recruitment?
faq.answer: The application process for NIRDPR recruitment is strictly online. Candidates need to visit the official career portal of NIRDPR to fill out and submit their application forms.

faq.question: Q8. What are the selection criteria for the NIRDPR posts?
faq.answer: The selection process for NIRDPR recruitment typically involves shortlisting of candidates based on their qualifications and experience, followed by a written test and/or an interview.

faq.question: Q9. Is there any work experience required for these positions?
faq.answer: Yes, work experience is required for all the positions. The minimum years of experience range from 2 to 10 years, depending on the specific post and its responsibilities, as detailed in the eligibility criteria.

faq.question: Q10. Where can I find the official NIRDPR recruitment notification?
faq.answer: The official NIRDPR recruitment notification can be accessed through the 'Important Links' section of this article. It is recommended to download and read the PDF carefully before applying.
